taxonomy: Grastidium agusanense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Grastidium and is a type of grass. Further, it is classified as a monocot, which means it has one cotyledon in its seedlings. This plant is native to the Philippines and is a part of the tropical flora in the region. As a member of the Poaceae family, Grastidium agusanense shares characteristics with other grasses.
